perturbation.original_text: The extent of the Confidential Information to be di sclosed will at all times be at the discretion of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: The extent of the Confidential Information to be disclosed will be limited to only the information pertaining to the specific financial and investment opportunities explicitly identified by both parties in writing beforehand.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by limiting the scope of 'Confidential Information' to pre-approved topics, which conflicts with the original clause that allowed the disclosing party full discretion. This affects the enforcement, as the receiving party can argue that information outside the pre-approved list is not covered by the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The extent of the Confidential Information to be disclosed will be limited to only the information pertaining to the specific financial and investment opportunities explicitly identified by both parties in writing beforehand.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants the disclosing party discretion over what Confidential Information is shared, while the changed text limits this discretion to only information pertaining to pre-approved topics. This directly contradicts the original clause. The contradicted text provided is the same as the changed text and helps further establish the point that there is a contradiction from what the original stated.

perturbation.original_text: Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as a secret and confidential and will not except with the prior written consent of the other, make use of the same except for the purpose of evaluation thereof, nor disclose the same to any third party except, and th en only to the extent necessary to evaluate the same, to such of its employees as shal l have been made fully aware that the same is secret and confidential and are bound t o treat it as such.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as confidential. However, either party may disclose such information to its parent company, subsidiaries, or Affiliates, without prior written consent, provided such entities have a demonstrable need to know.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original confidentiality clause by permitting disclosure to parent companies, subsidiaries, and Affiliates without prior consent. This affects enforcement, as it becomes unclear whether such disclosures are a breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as confidential. However, either party may disclose such information to its parent company, subsidiaries, or Affiliates, without prior written consent, provided such entities have a demonstrable need to know.
perturbation.justification: The changed text permits disclosure of confidential information to specific entities (parent company, subsidiaries, Affiliates) without prior written consent, which directly contradicts the original clause stating that confidential information will not be disclosed to any third party without prior written consent. The contradicted text section is the same as the changed text, and the explanation supports that there is a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiality in this agreemen t shall cease when both parties give written notification of its termination, or 5 years after the date hereof, whichever is the sooner.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiality in this agreement shall cease when both parties give written notification of its termination; however, for any trade secrets disclosed, the confidentiality obligations shall continue indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the confidentiality obligations cease after termination or 5 years, but then adds an exception that trade secrets remain confidential indefinitely. This creates uncertainty about when the confidentiality obligations actually end.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of confidentiality in this agreement shall cease when both parties give written notification of its termination; however, for any trade secrets disclosed, the confidentiality obligations shall continue indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text states that confidentiality obligations cease after a specific period (termination or 5 years). The changed text adds an exception for trade secrets, stating that those obligations continue indefinitely. This directly conflicts with the original statement and creates uncertainty about when confidentiality obligations actually end, adhering to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as it introduces conflicting obligations regarding the duration of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: The confidential information, including any documen ts, drawings, sketches, designs, materials or samples supplied hereunder, w ill remain the property of the party disclosing or supplying the same and no right s are granted to the other party in the same, whether patented or not except the limite d right to use set out above with respect to the said Field.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidential information, including any documents, drawings, sketches, designs, materials or samples supplied hereunder, will become the joint property of both parties upon the successful completion of the project XXXXX, granting each party equal rights to use and disclose the information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the confidential information becomes joint property upon project completion, which directly conflicts with the original clause that retains ownership with the disclosing party. This affects the enforcement of intellectual property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential information, including any documents, drawings, sketches, designs, materials or samples supplied hereunder, will become the joint property of both parties upon the successful completion of the project XXXXX, granting each party equal rights to use and disclose the information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that confidential information becomes joint property upon project completion, while the original text states that the disclosing party retains ownership. This directly contradicts the original ownership clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradiction text simply repeats the changed text. Thus, the change creates a conflict, and this meets the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All the additions to this agreement must be made in writing and must be executed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: All the additions to this agreement can be made via email confirmation from both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that additions to the agreement must be in writing and executed by both parties, while also stating that additions can be made via email confirmation. This creates uncertainty about the required method for amending the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All the additions to this agreement can be made via email confirmation from both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that all additions must be in writing and executed by both parties. The changed text states that additions can be made via email confirmation from both parties. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within the document because email confirmation does not satisfy the 'in writing and must be executed' requirement, and also because the contradicted text states the same thing as the changed text, meaning it also contradicts the original text. Therefore, it is a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
